title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. borbasiana
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. borbasiana, also known as Bugleweed or Borbas' Bugle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include acting as an antispasmodic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as having a sedative effect on the nervous system. The health benefits of Ajuga vulgaris subsp. borbasiana include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Bugleweed are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be found in some supplements and traditional medicines.
